summary: Partially decompiled the common fighter BarrelWait action by adding `ftCo_BarrelWait.c/.h` with most BarrelWait callbacks and helpers matched, while moving a large adjacent `ftCo_09CB` block out of `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` into a new C translation unit mostly as inline assembly. The PR report shows `ftCo_BarrelWait.c` at 99.87% file match, with `ftCo_8009EB18`, `ftCo_BarrelWait_IASA`, `ftCo_8009EC44`, and the empty Anim/Phys/Coll callbacks at 100%; only `ftCo_8009EC70` remained slightly off at 99.71%. `ftCo_09CB.c` was introduced as a split/relocation unit rather than a matched decompilation, with the report showing 0.00% for its functions.

key_files.note: New C file for BarrelWait; report says 99.87% file match, with several callbacks fully matching.
New header declaring BarrelWait action callbacks and static helpers with address comments.
New translation unit holding the 09CB range mostly as `ASM` inline assembly under `MUST_MATCH`, plus constants/string data needed by the moved block.
New header for the 09CB-range functions, moved out of `ft_08A4.h`.
Large assembly deletion from the 09CB/BarrelWait area, with remaining assembly adjusted for labels/data.
Refined `ftCommonData` layout around offsets 0x4DC and 0x5C0-0x600, including `Vec2`, `Vec3`, and `x5E0`.
Typed `ftColl_800788D4` and `ftColl_80079EA8` signatures to support the BarrelWait damage helper.
Header prototypes updated for `ftColl_800788D4(ftCo_GObj*)` and `ftColl_80079EA8(Fighter*, HitCapsule*, int)`.

what_changed: Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_BarrelWait.c` implementing `ftCo_8009EB18`, `ftCo_BarrelWait_Anim`, `ftCo_BarrelWait_IASA`, `ftCo_BarrelWait_Phys`, `ftCo_BarrelWait_Coll`, `ftCo_8009EC44`, and a partly matched `ftCo_8009EC70`.
Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_BarrelWait.h` with address-commented declarations for the new BarrelWait functions.
Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_09CB.c` and `ftCo_09CB.h`, moving many 09CB-range symbols out of the monolithic `ft_08A4` assembly/header area.
Deleted a large amount of assembly from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`; the changed-file summary records 2943 deleted lines and 407 added lines in that assembly file.
Registered the new `ftCo_09CB.c.o` and `ftCo_BarrelWait.c.o` build objects in `obj_files.mk` before the remaining `ft_08A4.s.o`.

Moved 09CB-range declarations such as `ftCo_8009CB40`, `ftCo_8009CF84`, `ftCo_8009DC54`, `ftCo_8009E0A8`, `ftCo_8009E0D4`, `ftCo_8009E140`, `ftCo_8009E318`, `ftCo_8009E714`, and `ftCo_8009E7B4` from `ft_08A4.h` into the new `ftCo_09CB.h`.
Added includes of `ftCommon/ftCo_09CB.h` to users such as `ftCo_08A6.c`, `ftCo_DamageIce.c`, `ftPr_Init.c`, `fighter.c`, and `ftaction.c`.
Refined `ftCommonData` fields: offset `0x4DC` became `Vec2 x4DC`, offset `0x4E4` became `Vec3 x4E4`, and the `0x5C0` region was split to expose fields including `int x5E0`.
Typed collision-library prototypes used by the new C code: `ftColl_800788D4(ftCo_GObj*)`, `ftColl_80079EA8(Fighter*, HitCapsule*, int)`, and `lbColl_80008D30(HitCapsule*, int)`.
Moved `ftDynamics_803C5720`, `ftDynamics_803C572C`, and `ftDynamics_803C5748` string definitions out of `ftdynamics.c` into the new `ftCo_09CB.c` context under `MWERKS_GEKKO`.
smart_moves: Split the work into a mostly matched gameplay file (`ftCo_BarrelWait.c`) and a separate 09CB-range staging file (`ftCo_09CB.c`) instead of forcing the entire neighboring assembly range to decompile at once.

Used `#if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP)` high-level C/`NOT_IMPLEMENTED` paths beside `#else` `nofralloc` inline assembly, preserving matching builds while allowing readable C attempts to live in the source.

Kept known source-level behavior for `ftCo_8009EC70` even though it was not perfect, while retaining exact inline assembly for `MUST_MATCH`; the PR report isolates the remaining mismatch to a 22/7700 score delta.
Introduced typed prototypes for collision helpers before using them in the BarrelWait damage path, reducing reliance on `UNK_PARAMS` in newly decompiled code.
Created a small `setCamData` inline helper to represent paired camera-box assignments from `ftCommonData` fields, supported by the `Vec2`/`Vec3` struct refinement.
Preserved stack-shape matching hints with explicit `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` unused stack arrays in `ftCo_8009EB18` and `ftCo_8009EC44`.
Kept address comments in the new headers, which makes future symbol searches and address-range ownership easier.
decomp_lessons: When a target action lives inside a larger unresolved assembly range, it can be productive to extract the whole range into C translation units first, leaving unresolved functions as inline assembly while matching the smaller action-specific file.
Do not leave newly decompiled code dependent on vague prototypes if the call signature is inferable from use sites; typing helper signatures can unlock cleaner matching and better field access.
Struct layout improvements should be made exactly where decompiled code needs semantic fields; here `ftCommonData` camera and damage-related offsets became usable by BarrelWait code.
For near-matches, keep both the readable C attempt and the exact `MUST_MATCH` assembly path so the repository gains documentation value without regressing matching builds.
Small unused-stack declarations can be a deliberate matching tactic for MWCC stack layout and should be guarded so they only affect `MUST_MATCH` builds.
Header ownership should follow the new module split: once the 09CB range became `ftCo_09CB.c`, its declarations were removed from the broader `ft_08A4.h` and centralized in `ftCo_09CB.h`.
naming_conventions: New common-fighter action files use the `ftCo_<Action>.c` and `ftCo_<Action>.h` pattern, as shown by `ftCo_BarrelWait.c/.h`.
Unidentified common-fighter functions remain address-based with the `ftCo_8009XXXX` naming style.
Headers use address-based include guards such as `GALE01_09EB18`, `GALE01_09CB40`, and `GALE01_00F9F8`.
Action callbacks follow the `ftCo_BarrelWait_Anim`, `ftCo_BarrelWait_IASA`, `ftCo_BarrelWait_Phys`, and `ftCo_BarrelWait_Coll` naming pattern.
Function declarations include address comments like `/* 09EC70 */` and sometimes retain `/* static */` annotations even in headers when the underlying symbol was understood as local/static in origin.
Unknown or not-yet-typed functions continue to use `UNK_RET` and `UNK_PARAMS`; newly understood helpers are upgraded to concrete types.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted removed `.s` functions into `ASM` functions with `nofralloc` inline assembly in C files under `MUST_MATCH`.
Used conditional compilation so non-`MUST_MATCH`/`WIP` builds can contain readable C or `NOT_IMPLEMENTED`, while exact assembly is still available for matching.
Added `#pragma peephole on` after inline assembly blocks where needed.
Used `#pragma force_active on` under MWERKS/WIP conditions for data or symbol retention.
Kept string/data symbols such as `ftDynamics_803C5720`, `ftDynamics_803C572C`, and `ftDynamics_803C5748` near the moved inline assembly to satisfy references and ordering.

For `ftCo_8009EC70`, represented game logic in C: copy fighter position, update mp collision and camera, load a `HitCapsule` through `lbColl_80008D30`, refresh collision, normalize/flip knockback angle, write damage fields, apply damage, then transition via `ftCo_8008DCE0(gobj, 91, 0)`.
Used explicit unused local stack byte arrays under `MUST_MATCH` to preserve stack layout in small functions.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context, so there is no direct reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match `ftCo_8009EC70` after the 22-point mismatch in PR905.
Find later PRs that decompile functions in `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_09CB.c` beyond inline assembly.
Search for subsequent renames or typing improvements to `ftCo_8009CB40`, `ftCo_8009CF84`, and `ftCo_8009EAF8`.
Check whether `ftCommonData` fields `x4DC`, `x4E4`, and `x5E0` received semantic names in later work.
Look for follow-up cleanup around `ftDynamics_803C5720`, `ftDynamics_803C572C`, and `ftDynamics_803C5748` after their relocation into `ftCo_09CB.c`.
